LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT VINA CAB, LLC, TIM NGUYEN DBA VINA CAB'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS DENIED. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: SUZANNE R. BOLANOS, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR	711, CKAREN@SFTC.ORG
REPLY BRIEF TO PLAINTIFFS OPPOSITION TO MOTION TO STRIKE PUNITIVE DAMAGE ALLEGATIONS FROM PLAINTIFFS COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 100030745) FILED BY DEFENDANT KUMAR, RAHUL

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT RAHUL KUMAR'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT, AS TO THE PUNITIVE DAMAGES ALLEGATIONS IN THE COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF SIDNEY CASTRO IS DENIED. BY ORDER FILED JULY 11, 2017, JUDGE BOLANOS DENIED VINA CAB, LLC AND TIM NGUYEN'S MOTION TO STRIKE THE PUNITIVE DAMAGES ALLEGATIONS IN MR. CASTRO'S COMPLAINT. MR. KUMAR FAILS TO EXPLAIN WHY THERE SHOULD BE A DIFFERENT RESULT ON HIS MOTION. LIBERALLY CONSTRUING HIS COMPLAINT, MR. CASTRO HAS ADEQUATELY ALLEGED THAT MR. KUMAR DROVE A VEHICLE AT A TIME THAT HE KNEW IT WAS UNSAFE FOR HIM TO DO SO AND THAT HE POSED A SERIOUS RISK OF HARM TO OTHERS WHO WERE IN THE VICINITY OF HIS VEHICLE. THIS IS SUFFICIENT TO SHOW THAT MR. KUMAR ACTED DESPICABLY AND WITH A WILLFUL AND CONSCIOUS DISREGARD OF THE RIGHTS AND SAFETY OF OTHERS. THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER THAT TRACKS THE COURT'S RULING, VERBATIM AND THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: ANN DELGADO, CSR NO. 11185, E-MAIL: ADELGADO@SFTC.ORG. (L&M302)

DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT RAHUL KUMAR'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO WRITTEN DISCOVERY; REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS. NO APPEARANCES. THOSE WHO HAVE NOT MADE AN APPEARANCE ARE DEEMED TO HAVE STIPULATED TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: THE MOTION IS UNOPPOSED AND IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF SHALL, WITHIN 10 DAYS, RESPOND WITHOUT OBJECTIONS TO: DEFENDANT'S SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, SET TWO; AND REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET TWO. SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED AGAINST PLAINTIFF IN THE AMOUNT OF $660. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE PRO TEM: THOMAS COHEN, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT RAHUL KUMAR MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION [FOR TENTATIVE RULING ONLY] [PART 2 OF 2]OFF CALENDAR DUPLICATE ENTRY.

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT RAHUL KUMAR'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. THE COURT HAS PREVIOUSLY RULED THAT THE CAUSE OF ACTION IS WELL-PLED. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT REGARDING WHETHER DEFENDANT WAS CONSCIOUSLY AWARE THAT HIS ACTIONS CREATED A SUBSTANTIAL RISK OF TREMENDOUS HARM TO OTHERS. (SEE CIV. CODE 3294.) (SEE WORD MINUTES FOR FULL DETAILS) (AS FULLY REPORTED IN THE REPORTER'S NOTES/TRANSCRIPT) PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR#6185, TONYVAUGHN@YAHOO.COM, REPORTED.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Jul-16-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Aaron Hancock, Esq. 510-596-0888 of Bishop Barry for Plaintiff.
Celia Bernal, Esq. 916-565-6222 of Diepenbrock & Cotter LLP for Defendant RAHUL KUMAR.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court overrules Defendant's evidentiary objections and asks questions. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT RAHUL KUMAR's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.

The Court adopts tentatve ruling. Defendant Kumar's motion for summary adjudication is denied. The Court has previously ruled that the cause of action is well-pled. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether Defendant was consciously aware that his actions created a substantial risk of tremendous harm to others. (See Civ. Code 3294.) (see word minutes for full details) (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-05-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Aaron C. Hancock of the law offices of [ ] ( ) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Anthony R. Rossmiller of the law offices of Diepenbrock & Cotter LLP (916-565-6222) appears by telephone as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT RAHUL KUMAR'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT, as to the punitive damages allegations in the complaint filed by plaintiff Sidney Castro is DENIED. By order filed July 11, 2017, Judge Bolanos denied Vina Cab, LLC and Tim Nguyen's motion to strike the punitive damages allegations in Mr. Castro's complaint. Mr. Kumar fails to explain why there should be a different result on his motion. Liberally construing his complaint, Mr. Castro has adequately alleged that Mr. Kumar drove a vehicle at a time that he knew it was unsafe for him to do so and that he posed a serious risk of harm to others who were in the vicinity of his vehicle. This is sufficient to show that Mr. Kumar acted despicably and with a willful and conscious disregard of the rights and safety of others. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR No. 11185, e-mail: adelgado@sftc.org. (L&M302)

Off Calendar Aug-11-2017 Set For Order To Show Cause On Oct-17-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-11-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT VINA CAB, LLC, TIM NGUYEN DBA VINA CAB's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is denied. Under CCP 425.10(a)(1), plaintiff is simply obliged to plead a "statement of the facts constituting the cause of action, in ordinary and concise language." In addition, the court liberally reads the complaint pursuant to CCP 452. In this case, plaintiff alleges that defendants were aware of Mr. Kumar's unfitness and still allowed him to drive. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges that defendants acted with a conscious disregard for the safety of others to support the claim for punitive damages. (See Perkins v. Sup. Crt. (1981) 117 Cal.App.3d 1.) Order is signed. Judge: Suzanne R. Bolanos, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 711, ckaren@sftc.org =(302/SRB)
